sources: Government of Dubai General Budget Cycle 2026-2028
Dubai Department of Finance (DOF) 2026 Fiscal Outlook

2026.methodology: The 2026 forecast is derived from the approved USD 82.42B three-year expenditure plan. Revenue growth is projected at 10.5% compared to 2025, driven by full-scale corporate tax integration and D33 economic expansion projects. Infrastructure spending sees a significant increase to 48% to support the Dubai Metro Blue Line expansion and Al Maktoum International Airport development. Figures include a mandatory USD 1.36B deduction for general reserves. All figures converted from AED to USD at a fixed rate of 3.6725.

sources: Government of Dubai Department of Finance (DOF) 2024-2026 Budget Cycle
Dubai Economic Agenda D33 Strategic Projections

2025.methodology: Figures represent the medium-term budget plan (USD 67.15B total for 2024-2026). The 2025 estimates account for an approximate 7.5% growth in non-oil revenue driven by the D33 agenda and the expansion of the corporate tax base which became fully integrated into fiscal reporting by 2025. All figures converted from AED to USD at a fixed rate of 3.6725.

sources: Dubai Department of Finance (DOF) Official Budget 2024
Law No. (20) of 2023 regarding the General Budget of the Dubai Government

category_3.name: Value Added Tax, Excise Tax, and Corporate Tax

2024.methodology: Data extracted from the official 2024-2026 budget cycle approved by the Ruler of Dubai. Total revenue (USD 24.68B) includes USD 1.5B allocated to general reserves, leaving USD 23.18B for budget operations. Spending breakdown follows the sectoral distribution defined in the Dubai Strategic Plan 2030 and Economic Agenda D33. All figures converted from AED to USD at a fixed rate of 3.6725.
